method_atom: A nonequilibrium fluctuation estimator that recovers an equilibrium difference between two ensemble-weighting functions from an ensemble of trajectories driven by a time-varying control parameter, whose statistical convergence degrades with the irreversible (dissipated) cost of the driving. The method augments each trajectory with an auxiliary, artificially imposed flow/transport field that couples the evolving state variables to the changing control parameter, and re-derives an exact estimating identity valid under this augmented dynamics. When the flow field escorts the state along a near-reversible path, dissipation is suppressed and the estimator's variance and bias collapse.
object_atom: A model physical system whose microscopic configuration coordinates evolve under a time-dependent external switching parameter, for which one wants the equilibrium free-energy difference Delta F between two thermodynamic states via fast-switching molecular simulations.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the auxiliary-flow-augmented nonequilibrium trajectory estimator with an exact identity and dissipation-reduced convergence; the domain-specific part is the physical/molecular system and its equilibrium free-energy difference under thermodynamic switching.
